entries.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Technical Program Manager, Knowledge Systems
messages.message:
Two problems here that I've actually lived. One: I was Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, so the surge cadence and the distributed-ownership scramble aren't news to me. Two: the real headache with AI-assisted work isn't the output, it's that the output scatters. Decisions in one doc, tasks with no context, conversations that go nowhere and connect to nothing.

So I built the operating system before accelerating the work: source-of-truth structure, handoffs, QA, an orchestration layer that keeps RAG-adjacent work coherent instead of confetti. Wrote it up here: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Happy to walk you through how it'd map to Knowledge Systems.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Staff Technical Program Manager, CX Agent Experience
messages.message:
Agent Cockpit is exactly the kind of thing I like: high-stakes internal platform, real users who need it working every single day, and a pile of upstream dependencies that turn into surprises if nobody's watching. I was Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, so surges, the intensity, and the cross-team scramble aren't new to me.

Most of what I do is keep the roadmap, dependencies, and launch-readiness checks connected instead of scattered across a dozen docs. I built a whole operating methodology around that problem for a confidential product: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Same instinct applies here, minus the AI hype.

Happy to talk through how I'd run intake and risk for the team.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Brand Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA • New York, NY • United States
messages.message:
The Brand Producer post is basically my last fifteen years in a trench coat. I ran creative production for Nike and Trek at AKQA, scoped and shipped a Q4 brand activation for Mozilla's Open What You Want, and led the Bored Ape Yacht Club redesign with an external studio, XFN wishlists and all: https://www.randallfransen.com/work/bored-ape-yacht-club-website-redesign

My actual specialty is the boring, valuable part: building the timeline and source-of-truth so your creatives stay in the creative and don't drown in feedback loops. Onboarding freelancers and agencies without the wheels coming off is a good day for me. Happy to talk.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Yuga’s property Bored Ape Yacht Club was still rocking it’s original design and functionality in 2023.

workExampleSelection.title: BORED APE YACHT CLUB | Website Redesign

messages.title: Senior Programs & Business Operations Lead, Airbnb Services
messages.message: I've been inside Airbnb before as an EP on contract, so the shape of taking something from concept to launch across cross-functional teams isn't new terrain for me. The part of this that fits me cleanly: standing up the programs and processes so a new category actually scales instead of scattering. I did that at Coinbase as Production Lead for Base, and again running a $14MM retainer for HTC with a lean team of four. Building the operating system before you accelerate the work is a habit of mine, not a slide. Happy to walk through how I'd approach the emerging-category side. -Randall

messages.title: Product Operations Manager
messages.message:
The launch coordination part of this is my whole day job, just under different logos: coordinating end-to-end launches, exit criteria, early access, the cross-functional herding between product, legal, support, all of it. I ran a $14MM retainer with a team of four at Swift and led the Base launch at Coinbase.

The agentic-workflow piece is where I've been living lately. I built an actual operating system for AI-assisted work: source of truth, handoffs, QA, an orchestration agent so the output stops scattering. It's here: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Happy to talk shop about how it'd map to Notion.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Creative Director, Growth
messages.message:
Growth creative that ships fast without turning into a pile of disconnected docs and half-finished tests. That's the part I've lived. At AKQA I was Program Director on Nike and Trek, and I ran production for Base at Coinbase, so I know how to steer agencies and multidisciplinary teams toward stronger work without slowing them down.

On the AI side, I built P.H.R.E.D., an operating system that keeps AI-assisted work connected instead of scattered: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred. Turns out the hard part of AI work isn't the output, it's where the output ends up.

Worth a conversation?

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Marketing Events Producer
messages.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
messages.message: Run-of-show, cue sheets, calling the show live: that's the part of production I like best, when the plan meets a room full of people and something's always about to go sideways. Fifteen-plus years of digital and live production, including a stretch as Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, where AI-and-crypto stage moments had to actually land with skeptical audiences, not just look good. I've also run the vendor and budget side (managed a $14MM retainer at Swift, team of four) and built the process scaffolding that keeps small teams from drowning. Producing how Anthropic shows up on stage sounds like a good place to point that at. Worth a conversation?

messages.title: Strategy & Operations Manager, Consumer Revenue
messages.message:
Straight up: I'm a producer by trade, not a strategic finance hire, so if SQL-and-modeling-from-scratch is the whole bar, I'm not your person. But the rest of this role is what I actually do. Standing up process where none exists, defining red lines, and getting deals from signed term sheet through Legal and Accounting without direct authority.

At Coinbase I led production for Base sitting between Product, BD, and Finance. At AKQA I ran a $14MM retainer and the messy cross-functional workflows underneath it. Deal desk, approval frameworks, and codifying policy as a function matures is familiar ground. Happy to talk if the ops half of this is where you're feeling the pain.

messages.title: Professional Services Program Manager
messages.message:
The bit that jumps out here: turning transformation goals into execution plans that don't scatter across fifteen docs and three Slack threads. That's most of what I do. At AKQA as Program Director I ran multi-workstream engagements for Nike and Trek, and at Coinbase I was Production Lead for Base, keeping executive, technical, and delivery folks aligned on the same milestones.

The structure piece is where I actually nerd out. I built P.H.R.E.D., a source-of-truth operating system that keeps complex work connected instead of everywhere at once: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Remote-async, ownership, clear docs. That's my natural habitat. Happy to talk.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Viral Creative Producer
messages.message: The Brian-in-a-glass-box run and the Penny funeral are the kind of swing I like: earn attention, don't rent it. I've spent 15+ years as an EP running stunts and activations end to end, concept through vendors, permits, capture, and distribution. For Mozilla's Open What You Want I built a Q4 activation to spark a Gen Z-coded moment punching up at Big Tech, digital plus IRL over a three-month run: https://www.randallfransen.com/work/billionaire-blast-off-mozilla. I know how to protect the one detail that makes a stunt land and let the rest go. Happy to bring the operational rigor behind your next big one.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Create a Gen Z-coded moment to amplify Open What You Want.

messages.title: Head of Copy, Creative Studio
messages.location: New York City, NY; San Francisco, CA
messages.message:
Straight up: I'm not your copy lead. I don't write the voice, I build the thing that keeps the voice from scattering across forty docs and a graveyard of dead AI threads.

Your listing has a line about being connective tissue between human creative vision and AI execution infrastructure. That's the part I've actually built. On a confidential mobile app with two people and a brutal timeline, I stood up an operating system before we touched acceleration: source of truth, handoffs, QA, an orchestration agent keeping AI-assisted work connected instead of everywhere. https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

Before that: Base at Coinbase, a Bored Ape redesign, Program Director at AKQA. If the Creative Studio needs someone to make the scale actually run under the writers, that's me.

insertedExample.oneHitter: Project Hub for Retrieval, Execution, & Delivery

messages.title: Chief of Staff, to the CPO
messages.message:
The line in this that jumped out: turning the People org into an AI-first function without the output scattering to twelve docs nobody reads. I built an operating methodology for exactly that on a confidential product, small team, aggressive timeline. Structure first, then acceleration, humans in control of scope and decisions: https://lab26.randallfransen.com/phred

On the operating-cadence side, I've run the room for a senior leader before. Program Director at AKQA on Nike and Trek, Production Lead for Base at Coinbase, a $14MM retainer with a team of four. I keep planning, reporting, and priorities pointed the same direction while staying out of the way. Happy to talk if the fit's real.
